scenario: Helios Insurance evaluates risk windows on Camunda 8.8 across 6000 policies per day. A modeller writes a sequence flow condition that should be true only when the variable age is in the closed range from 30 to 40. The team wants the FEEL form documented on the boolean expressions page that compares a number to a closed numeric range without writing two separate comparisons.
question: Which FEEL expression on the boolean expressions page returns true when age is within the closed range from 30 to 40?

options.text: age in range(30, 40); the docs name range() as the canonical FEEL range constructor for closed numeric intervals and recommend it instead of the and operator for readability in sequence flow conditions on exclusive gateways
age between 30 and 40; the docs define between [x] and [y] as the same as (_ >= x and _ <= y) for numbers, strings, dates, times, date-times, and durations on the boolean expressions page
age within [30..40]; the docs introduce within as a dedicated keyword for closed intervals on numbers, strings, dates, times, date-times, and durations on the boolean expressions page
age contained by interval(30, 40, closed); the docs define interval() as the FEEL constructor that returns a closed numeric range and contained by as the comparison keyword to test membership

a.text: Incorrect. The boolean expressions page does not list a range() constructor or document it as the canonical closed-range form; the documented comparison for a closed range is between [x] and [y], defined as the same as (_ >= x and _ <= y).

b.text: Correct. The Comparison section of the boolean expressions page lists between [x] and [y] as a comparison operator that is defined as the same as (_ >= x and _ <= y) and supports number, string, date, time, date-time, and duration values, so age between 30 and 40 matches the closed range from 30 to 40.

c.text: Incorrect. The boolean expressions page does not list a within keyword; the closed-range comparison documented in the Comparison section is between [x] and [y], defined as the same as (_ >= x and _ <= y).

d.text: Incorrect. The boolean expressions page does not document an interval() constructor or a contained by keyword; the documented closed-range comparison is between [x] and [y] applied directly to the value.

explanation: The Comparison section on the FEEL boolean expressions page lists between [x] and [y] as a comparison operator equivalent to (_ >= x and _ <= y). It supports number, string, date, time, date-time, and duration values, so age between 30 and 40 returns true when age is in the closed range from 30 to 40.
